title: Gloomful Island
wikitext:
{{Warning}}
{{Class_Variable|banner-text=white|color-l=30%|class=Deadzone|hexagon=🕱|safety=Worst Percent of Safety and Security|security=Extremely Unhabitable|entity=Terribly Dangerous on Mental Health}}
'''Gloomful island''' is an anomalous level in The Backrooms.
{{Blurimage|file=gloomy island bay.jpg|caption=The first image ever taken of '''Gloomful Island'''.}}
{{Blurbackground|file=gloomy island bay.jpg}}
== Description ==
'''Gloomful Island''' is an anomalous and heavily restricted island within the Backrooms that can only be accessed through emotional triggers related to overwhelming joy. Unlike other levels that require exploration, travel, or noclipping, Gloomful Island manifests solely as a reaction to intense emotional highs. Wanderers who laugh too hard, feel too safe, or reach inner peace after years of suffering may suddenly find themselves surrounded by dense gray mist, standing on the island’s cold shoreline.

The island itself spans approximately 12 kilometers in diameter, though attempts to measure its geography consistently fail. Distances warp subtly, and attempts to map the terrain often result in walkers looping back to their original location without realizing they turned. GPS devices fail to acquire a signal, and analog methods are rendered useless by unpredictable terrain shifts. The island does not appear on any known coordinate systems and cannot be found intentionally.
{{Blurimage|file=Gloomful Island Sun.png|caption=The second image of '''Gloomful Island'''.}} {{Blurbackground|file=gloomy island bay.jpg}}
The climate is unnaturally stable. The temperature remains fixed at exactly 12°C at all times, regardless of time, season, or location. Despite this constant chill, there is no breeze, no snowfall, and no seasonal change. Clothing remains damp from near-total humidity, and fog never lifts. This constant wetness clings to skin and fabric alike, and over time, subjects report the sensation of something breathing lightly against their necks, despite the complete absence of wind.

There is no sunlight. The sky above the island is a motionless mass of thick gray clouds, which emit a flat ambient light that illuminates everything without casting shadows. There is no indication of a sun, moon, or any celestial object. The sky does not change in brightness or color, and the concept of day or night becomes meaningless. Some survivors have described this lack of natural timekeeping as more damaging than physical hunger.

The island is divided into several distinct environmental zones, each exhibiting a unique emotional or perceptual influence. The coastline consists of black, oily silt that sticks to anything it touches. There are no waves, and the surrounding sea is utterly motionless. The water is thick and reflective only from a distance. When approached, it turns matte and dark, absorbing light instead of bouncing it back. Those who attempt to swim into it vanish without sound or trace.

Moving inland, the Fogwood covers a large portion of the central island. The forest consists of dead, barkless trees that stretch high into the sky. Their limbs hang downward like limp arms. The forest is unusually quiet. Footsteps make no sound on the soft ground, and voices become muted. Even thoughts begin to slow in this region, and many wanderers describe forgetting their name, purpose, or reason for continuing forward while within its interior.

Beyond the Fogwood, the land rises into the region known as Grave Hills. Dozens of rolling hills are dotted with simple gravestones carved from dark stone. None have names, dates, or symbols. Each stone is unique in shape, though they all seem hand-carved. Standing among them evokes intense feelings of guilt, loss, or suppressed grief. Many wanderers break down while walking through this area, often forgetting what they were doing moments before.

On the western edge lies Hollow Field, a large clearing of tall, brittle grass. The plants here appear colorless, and stepping through them produces a faint whispering sound, even without wind. In this field, people often report seeing distant figures walking just beyond the fog. These apparitions resemble lost loved ones or idealized memories but vanish when approached. Time also appears to behave erratically in this zone. A few minutes may feel like hours, or vice versa.

The final major location is the Dock. A narrow wooden path stretches into the sea, terminating in a broken lantern bound in rusted chains. Despite its appearance, the lantern has never lit on its own. However, there are unconfirmed reports of it glowing for those who resist despair for exactly three days. The dock is known as a place of finality. Some wanderers walk to the end and leap into the ocean without speaking. Others simply disappear before reaching the edge.

No wildlife exists on Gloomful Island. There are no birds, no insects, no animal sounds, and no decomposition. Even organic matter such as leaves or bark does not rot in the traditional sense. It simply fades, desaturating until it vanishes entirely. Attempts to introduce new life, such as planting seeds or bringing pets, result in immediate failure. Flora from other levels either wilts or dissolves after a few minutes in the island's atmosphere.

Water on the island is unsafe for consumption. All puddles and pools are murky gray or brown, emitting a metallic odor. Drinking it may cause auditory hallucinations or involuntary memory flashes, often of childhood events or moments of vulnerability. In rare cases, puddles act as reflective portals that show moments of happiness, but only in reverse. Attempts to collect samples have failed, as the liquid becomes inert or evaporates once removed from its origin.

Gloomful Island is not a level that can be entered by force or intent. It is reactive. It does not appear to those who seek it or those in despair, but to those who feel too much joy. In this sense, it is not a place but a correction. The island is the emotional gravity of the Backrooms, balancing every smile with silence. There are no recorded permanent structures, no tools, and no hope. The land itself is the entity. It does not hunt. It waits.

The whole point of this level is to show that the Backrooms are not meant to be fun.

== Entities ==

=== The Organism ===
Unlike most Backrooms levels, Gloomful Island is the entity itself. No other entities have ever been confirmed. Researchers believe every tree, patch of fog, puddle, and shoreline is part of a single living organism, although no central body has ever been found.

Rather than attacking physically, the island manipulates the minds of wanderers. It gradually learns their memories and emotions before using them against them.

Its most dangerous ability is perfectly mimicking the voices of loved ones, whether they are alive or deceased. The voices come from the fog, trees, or sea, calling victims by name or luring them deeper into the island.
The island also reacts to a wanderer's emotions. The fog thickens, paths shift, and groups are easily separated. For these reasons, Gloomful Island is classified as both a level and a single sentient entity.

== Entrances And Exits ==

=== Entrances ===

* When a wanderer experiences intense happiness, joy, or comfortable, an angel-like entity may appear and transport them to '''Gloomful Island'''.

=== Exits ===

* If a wanderer reaches the point of complete emotional collapse and intentionally ends their life, there is a rare chance they may see a hand emerging from the soil or puddle beneath them. If touched, the hand will pull the wanderer back to the level they were in prior to arriving at 
* Drowning oneself in the motionless black sea surrounding the island may result in relocation to [[Level 7]], though some who attempt this are never seen again. Those who survive describe awakening within the sunken halls of [[Level 7]], still soaked and unable to recall their own name for several hours.
* Starving to death or allowing oneself to fade through neglect has led some wanderers to reappear in [[Level 0]]. This process is agonizingly slow and not always successful. Survivors often return with long-term memory damage and reduced emotional capacity.
